what_it_does: A noticeboard design note warning that a tree-only, node-only selection model is too restrictive for real reviewing behavior and arguing that the envelope anchor substrate must support node, point, and character-range anchors.
observations: Warns against making the block hierarchy the only selection primitive
Identifies three anchor kinds: node, point, and character-range across nodes
Recommends keeping the subtree gesture as the default while making the envelope anchor expressive
Cites BD-K calling the envelope 'the lynchpin'
States that retrofitting anchor flexibility later is likely to force a rewrite
Uses 'Tim' as a user persona who will be frustrated by an overly rigid model
standouts: Retrofitting anchor-flexibility later forces a rewrite
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter with id, address, type, source, state, title, author_session, channel, links, and timestamps
Markdown section heading numbered as part of a parent item
References a parent item via part_of link
notes: A design warning that the selection/anchoring envelope must support points and ranges, not just tree nodes, and should be designed into the schema early.
questions: What is the exact envelope schema being proposed?
What is the parent item-a72b23c2 and the broader thread context?
Is 'Tim' a specific user persona or a generic example?
Which existing selection model is being critiqued?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the stated purpose is to warn against a tree-only selection model, and the content does exactly that while prescribing a more flexible anchor design.
apparent_themes: Anchor abstraction as a substrate concern
intention_signals: To influence the envelope/anchor data model early so it can name spans, points, and ranges as naturally as nodes, before implementation becomes expensive to change.
novelty: Frames selection primitives as three distinct anchor kinds (node, point, character-range) unified through a single envelope, and elevates the envelope as the 'lynchpin' of the substrate.
contribution: The requirement that the selection substrate support node, point, and character-range anchors, plus the rationale that anchor flexibility should be built into the envelope schema early rather than retrofitted.
summary_for_embedding: A noticeboard design note arguing that a selection/anchoring envelope must support node, point, and character-range anchors rather than a tree-only node model, and that this flexibility should be built into the schema early.
intention_for_embedding: To persuade the team to encode three kinds of anchors into the envelope substrate before the selection model hardens.
